title: Pros & Cons — Travel vs Work (Summer 2021)
html:
TL;DR

  • April 2021 decision-doc: spend 3 summer months traveling backpacker-style OR commit 100% to a (likely unpaid) crypto internship
  • Travel candidate continents: West Africa (Nigeria, Senegal — total novelty, language barrier) or South America (Mexico → Colombia → Brazil — language advantage)
  • Working candidate: a foothold in crypto, framed as "earn-money path" vs "earn-knowledge path" — Yahir explicitly picks the knowledge path if it pans out
  • Quotes Dad: "Learn to run before you can fly" — used to justify the working option
  • Cites Yes Theory's "seek discomfort" framing as travel's main pull
  • Resolution plan: cold-email crypto contacts first; if nothing pans out by deadline, default to backpacking Africa

Notes

A short typing-out-thoughts dilemma doc from April 2021, post-high-school senior year and pre-OSU freshman summer. The two paths Yahir is weighing:

Travel — 2–3 months pure backpacking, "no turning back," chasing perspective and a story to tell. He rules out Europe (pandemic-era expensive) and India (described as "absolute fuckfest" — likely the Delta-wave moment). West Africa vs South America are the finalists; South America wins on language, Africa wins on novelty.

Work — Earn money OR earn knowledge. He explicitly devalues the earn-money path ("essentially repeating what I was doing last year") and frames the earn-knowledge path as foundation-building for "starting my own business" — first appearance of the crypto career thesis that later powers the ELI internship narrative and the Corvallis Crypto Club founding.

The decision tree he commits to at the end of the doc: reach out to crypto contacts and parents-friends first; if no internship lands, default to Africa backpacking. Historically, neither happened — the CIEE/YES program South Africa trip from junior year is the closest analogue, and his Peru trip later that summer is what filled the gap.

Connects to: 2023 01 30 eli internship, 2021 07 04 renaissance scholarship 1.
